sections.paragraph:
-

(1) to close, to shut up, Jer 32:2, Jer 32:3 Psal. 88:9 . Intrans. to be closed, Hag 1:10.

(2) to restrain, to hold in, Num 11:28 Ecc 8:8 Psal. 40:10 followed by מִן from doing anything, 1Sa 25:33 Psa 119:101 followed by מִן of pers. to withhold something from some one, to prohibit in respect to anything, Gen 23:6 Psa 40:12 comp. Hag 1:10. (Ch., Syr. כְּלָא ܟܠܳ, Eth. ከልእ፡ to prohibit, Arab. كأا to guard, II. to prohibit, to restrain. This root is also very widely extended in the western languages, in the signification of shutting up: κλείω, κλείς, κλήϊς, κλαΐς, clavis, claudo; in the signification of prohibiting: κωλύω, κολούω, κολάζω, Compare also celo, occulo.)

Niphal, to be shut up, restrained, Gen 8:2 Exo 36:6.

The forms are often borrowed from the cognate verb כָּלָה, which see; as כְּלִתִנִי 1Sa 25:33, כָּלוּ 1Sa 6:10, יִכְלֶה Gen 23:6, and כָּלִאתִי Psa 119:101. On the other hand, כַּלֵּא Dan 9:24, inf. Piel is for כַּלֵּה or כַּלֹּות (Lehrg. page 418).

Derivatives, כְּלוּא, כְּלִיא, מִכְלָה, מִכְלָאֹות, and-
